name: Home Style Pork and Cucumber Soup
recipeIngredient: 1/2 tbsp Rice wine or dry sherry
1 tsp Vegetable oil or Asian-sesame oil
1 quart Slice fresh ginger, lightly crushed
1 tbsp Minced Smithfield ham or Prosciutto (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Nothing fancy about this soup—it's just simple, delicious, and refreshing.
2. Cut the pork into thin slices and combine with the sugar, soy sauce, wine, cornstarch, and oil. Set aside.
3. Peel, halve, and seed the cucumber. Cut crosswise into 1/4-inch-thin slices.
4. Bring stock and ginger to a boil.
5. Add the pork mixture and reduce to a lively simmer; simmer for 10 minutes.
6. Add the cucumber and simmer until translucent, about 3 minutes.
7. Garnish with the optional ham and coriander.
8. Serves 6 as part of a multiple-dish Chinese meal.
Per Serving:
65 calories, 6 g protein, 4 g carbohydrate, 2 g fat (1 g saturated), 13 mg cholesterol, 101 mg sodium, 0 g fiber.
